meaning: adj.在下面的,根本的,潜在的,[商]优先的
examples: The underlying trend of inflation is still upwards.
To stop a problem you have to understand its underlying causes.
Unemployment may be an underlying cause of the rising crime rate.
The textbook explanation is that there is an underlying, magical essence that only good brands share.
The fact is, almost all other underlying data point to weaker, not stronger jobs numbers.
